bugs.error_message: V4 controller: combat damage never resolved â€” attacking dealt no damage
bugs.root_cause: V4 removed all manual step controls and left only PASS. advance_step from combat_damage to end_of_combat does not call resolve_combat_damage, and V4 never invoked the RPC, so passing priority through combat_damage skipped damage entirely.
bugs.fix: Added combat damage resolution to PriorityPanel: during combat_damage step the active player sees a Resolve button that calls resolve_combat_damage. Tracks returned damage_stage (first_strike vs regular) to handle the two-pass first/double strike flow; switches back to PASS once the regular pass resolves. Resets stage on leaving the step.

bugs.error_message: V4: after declaring blockers the game is stuck â€” DeclareBlockersLayout stays shown and step never advances
bugs.root_cause: layoutState returned declare_blockers whenever step===declare_blockers AND incomingAttackers>0, without checking priority. After the defender confirmed and passed priority, step was still declare_blockers with attackers present, so the layout kept showing and trapped them â€” neither player could complete the priority pass to advance. The declare_attackers layout already gated on hasPriority; declare_blockers did not.
bugs.fix: Added && hasPriority to the declare_blockers layoutState condition. After the defender passes, priority leaves them, the layout exits to the board (Wait), and the active player can pass to advance to combat_damage.

bugs.root_cause: scale: 0.85, opacity: 0 â†’ 0.9, opacity: 0

Centering used Tailwind -translate-x-1/2 -translate-y-1/2, but the panel is a Framer motion.div with animate={{scale}}, which writes an inline transform that overrides the Tailwind translate utilities. The centering translate was lost so the top-left corner sat at screen center.
bugs.fix: Center via flexbox on the backdrop (flex items-center justify-center) and make the panel a child of it. The panel keeps its scale/opacity animation without needing transform-based positioning.

bugs.error_message: V4: cannot cast creature/player-targeting spells (Lightning Strike, Giant Growth) â€” no Cast button; plus Zod warning on array target_type
bugs.root_cause: getCanQuickCast gates any instant/sorcery whose oracle text contains the word target on pendingStackCount>0 (via doesCardRequireStackTarget hasTargetText), which is only correct for counterspells. Damage/pump spells target a creature/player, not a stack item, so canCast was false and no Cast button rendered. Separately, the V1 Zod schema allowed only string target_type, but the seeded Lightning Strike uses an array [creature,player], producing a dev-mode validation warning.
bugs.fix: In CardActionSheet, compute canCast from spellPlan: damage/pump spells are castable at their natural speed (instant=hasPriority, sorcery=main phase) regardless of stack; only counterspell/normal use getCanQuickCast. Schema: target_type now accepts string or string[] and unknown V1 actions allow power/toughness fields (pump).

bugs.error_message: Route "/cards/behavior": Uncached data or `connection()` was accessed outside of `<Suspense>`. (Next 16 cacheComponents) at SupabaseAuthClient.getClaims in CardBehaviorPage
bugs.root_cause: Server page called `await supabase.auth.getClaims()` at the top level of the route component. Under Next 16 cacheComponents, uncached request-time data must be read inside a <Suspense> boundary or it blocks the whole route.
bugs.fix: Moved getClaims()+redirect into a child async server component (CardBehaviorContent) wrapped in <Suspense>, mirroring app/controller/[id]/page.tsx and app/protected/page.tsx.

bugs.error_message: V4 combat soft-locks after attacking with a creature that has an 'attacks' triggered ability: the trigger resolves mid declare_attackers, priority returns to the active player, and the declare-attackers picker re-appears with no way to advance.
bugs.root_cause: layoutState showed 'declare_attackers' whenever step==declare_attackers && active player has priority. The new attacks trigger (migration 077) enqueues a triggered_ability on the stack at declaration; resolving it (pass_priority round) resets priority_player_id to the active player while still in declare_attackers, so the attacker picker re-rendered. Re-declaring the already-declared attacker 400s (unique constraint), and the picker offered no pass affordance -> stuck.
bugs.fix: Compute myDeclaredAttackers = combatAssignments.filter(a => a.attacking_player_id === playerId) and require myDeclaredAttackers.length === 0 in the declare_attackers layoutState condition. Once attackers are declared, fall through to the default layout whose PriorityPanel exposes a Pass button, letting the step advance to declare_blockers.

bugs.error_message: An 'any target' damage trigger was silently dropped when no creature was on the battlefield; and 'destroy target creature an opponent controls' let you destroy your own creature.
bugs.root_cause: Migration 080's trigger_effect_requires_creature_target used behavior_target_type_matches, which returns true for 'any'/['creature','player']. So any-target damage triggers were classified as needing a creature target and dropped by enqueue when no creature existed. Separately, targeted triggers/spells ignored 'an opponent controls' / 'you control' restrictions.
bugs.fix: Added behavior_target_type_is_creature_only (creature-only check) so any-target triggers fall through to apply_triggered_ability_effects' each_opponent default. Added target_controller (any|opponent|you) enforced via session_has_targetable_creature + creature_target_controller_ok in enqueue, choose_triggered_ability_creature_target, resolve_top_of_stack guard, and put_action_on_stack; mirrored client-side (creatureMatchesController/readTargetController filter pickers, SpellPlan.targetController, put-on-stack targetController param).

bugs.error_message: ERROR: relation "public.game_cards" does not exist (SQLSTATE 42P01) during `npx supabase start` / `db reset`, at statement 0 of 202605010000_move_card_to_zone.sql (`update public.game_cards`)
bugs.root_cause: The migration set is incomplete: base tables public.cards, public.game_cards, and public.game_players are never created by any migration (no `create table`, no `alter ... add column`). They were created out-of-band on the hosted project (dashboard/bootstrap), so migrations assume they already exist. A clean local `db reset` from zero has no such tables, so the first migration that touches game_cards fails. Independent of migrations 079-086.
bugs.fix: RESOLVED. A surgical baseline of just the missing tables was NOT enough: the incremental chain also can't replay from scratch (get_session_players/get_stack_items/get_combat_assignments/get_turn_state change return type via create-or-replace -> 42P13; and Supabase keys migrations by leading version digits, so mid-chain drop-shims collide on schema_migrations PK and can't sort between consecutive versions). Final fix = SQUASH for local: `npx supabase db dump --schema public` -> supabase/migrations/00000000000000_baseline.sql (full schema, reproduces prod incl. 079-086); moved the 88 incremental files to supabase/migrations_archive/ (Supabase ignores them, kept as history); added local-only 00000000000001_local_test_relax_fks.sql dropping the auth.users/profiles FK chain so the harness uses throwaway UUIDs. `supabase db reset` now succeeds and the harness proving trio passes.

bugs.error_message: In-game scry/Opt spell did nothing (no scry decision); a scry+draw spell created a draw_cards stack item and only drew.
bugs.root_cause: getSpellPlan checked the single-action draw case before the spell_effect (scry/surveil/multi-action) case, so Opt = [scry,draw] matched draw first and returned kind:"draw", dropping the scry. The engine/DB were correct; the client mis-classified the spell.
bugs.fix: Moved the spell_effect detection (allUntargeted && (hasScrySurveil || actions.length>1)) ABOVE the draw single-action check in getSpellPlan so multi-action/scry spells route to cast_spell_effect.

bugs.error_message: After a scry/surveil parked for a decision, passing priority advanced the step/round and players kept passing — the game moved on with an unresolved decision.
bugs.root_cause: A parked stack item is status=awaiting_decision (not pending). pass_priority counts only pending items, so it saw an empty stack and called advance_step, and the client pass button was not blocked for pending decisions.
bugs.fix: pass_priority now raises if any game_pending_decisions row is pending for the session (guard added at top). Client computes passBlockReason (Resolve your decision / Waiting for a decision / Choose trigger target) and disables Pass. submit_decision finalizes and resets priority cleanly.

bugs.error_message: fight tests F1/F5/F6 asserted the 4/4 fighter SURVIVES after fighting a Deathtouch Viper (1/1) — wrong once deathtouch was implemented (the viper's 1 damage is lethal via CR 702.2).
bugs.root_cause: apply_fight delegated damage to apply_creature_effect('deal_damage'), which only marked damage_marked and never set dealt_deathtouch_damage. So fight ignored the dealer's deathtouch and a 4/4 survived a deathtouch 1/1. The tests were written against that incorrect behavior (used the Deathtouch Viper as a plain 1/1).
bugs.fix: mig 103: apply_creature_effect deal_damage honours an optional 'deathtouch' param (OR-ing dealt_deathtouch_damage, mirroring combat); apply_fight captures both creatures' card_has_deathtouch up front (like powers, since fight is simultaneous) and passes it per hit. Updated F1/F5/F6 to use Silhana Ledgewalker (non-deathtouch 1/1) for the survivor scenarios; added F9 (deathtouch 1/1 kills a 0/4 wall, survives) and F10 (deathtouch defender kills the 4/4 too).

bugs.error_message: 5 builder characterization tests failed: parseScriptToForm returned null for search_library with filter:{type_line} (expected Form mode); exact-build crashed reading 'keywords' of null
bugs.root_cause: Added a 'name' sub-field to the search_library filter object in the registry. parseFields requires every non-optional sub-field to be present, so a filter carrying only type_line (no name) was rejected → JSON mode. The object serializer (fieldToJson) also emits EVERY sub-field with no empty-drop, so name:'' would leak into serialized tutors.
bugs.fix: Kept filter.name OUT of the guided-form registry — it's JSON/AI-authorable only (engine + Zod + LLM support it), matching the gain_control untap/haste and search_library tapped/reveal precedent. Reverted the registry filter to type_line-only and the defaultEffect test expectation back to {type_line:''}. The graveyard destination (a clean enum extension) stayed in the form.

bugs.error_message: new row for relation "game_stack_items" violates check constraint "game_stack_items_action_type_check" (23514) when casting the new multi_creature_effect action type
bugs.root_cause: game_stack_items.action_type has a CHECK constraint whitelisting allowed action types (last redefined in mig 107). Registering a handler/builder in stack_action_handlers is NOT enough — a new castable action_type must ALSO be added to this CHECK or the INSERT in put_action_on_stack fails.
bugs.fix: Added a drop+recreate of game_stack_items_action_type_check to mig 112, reproducing the mig 107 allow-list plus 'multi_creature_effect'.

bugs.error_message: current transaction is aborted, commands ignored until end of transaction block (25P02) in a feature test, after asserting an expected rejection
bugs.root_cause: The harness runs each test in ONE Postgres transaction (withRolledBackTx). When an RPC raises (the expected rejection), the whole tx is poisoned — any subsequent query (e.g. a post-assertion zoneOf) fails with 25P02. Asserting state AFTER an expected-throw in the same tx is impossible.
bugs.fix: Dropped the post-rejection state queries (MT5); the assert.rejects IS the assertion. Pattern: a test asserting a raise must not run further queries in the same tx (MT6 already followed this).

bugs.error_message: CO1/CO2 combat over-assignment tests flaky: "Must assign lethal damage to earlier blockers before later ones" intermittently / reject test intermittently passes
bugs.root_cause: Two blockers declared in the same tx get equal damage_assignment_order AND equal created_at, so resolve_combat_damage orders them by the id tiebreak (random uuid). The test assumed declaration order == resolution order, so over-assigning 4/0 to b1/b2 landed on the wrong blocker ~half the time and tripped (or skipped) the CR 510.1c lethal-before-later validation.
bugs.fix: Added blockerOrder(s,attacker) helper that queries game_combat_blockers ordered by (damage_assignment_order, created_at, id) — the exact order the engine uses — and assign by [first,second] from that, not declaration order.

bugs.error_message: HM3 monohybrid explicit-GENERIC test: expected pool.W 1, got 0
bugs.root_cause: pay_mana_cost auto-distributes generic cost across the pool in order C,W,U,B,R,G. Paying {2/W} as 2 generic with pool {W:1,R:2} legitimately spent the W first, so the test asserting W untouched was wrong (engine behaved correctly).
bugs.fix: Funded the 2 generic with colourless {W:1,C:2} so the auto-generic spends C before W; assert C=0 and W=1.

bugs.root_cause: Missing or incorrect type annotation

bugs.error_message: Unsupported stack action type: spell_effect (resolve_top_of_stack); many tests failed after mig 123
bugs.root_cause: mig 123 reproduced resolve_top_of_stack AND enqueue_triggered_ability from supabase/local-bootstrap/00_baseline.sql, but the LIVE versions come from later migrations: resolve_top_of_stack was refactored to the registry dispatcher in mig 104, and enqueue_triggered_ability gained multi-target reach in mig 114/116. Copying the baseline reverted both — dropping spell_effect (and every registry action_type) resolution and multi-target triggers.
bugs.fix: Rebuilt mig 123 on the CURRENT definitions: enqueue from mig 116 + apnap_rank; resolve_top_of_stack from mig 104 (dispatcher) + the order_pending_triggers settle call. 316/316.

bugs.fix: Not fixed (out of scope). To repair: run the script as ESM (add a local package.json {"type":"module"} for scripts, or rename to .mts, or wrap the body in an async main() instead of top-level await). Zod validity of fixtures is currently covered indirectly by the engine tests that cast them.

bugs.error_message: protection.test.ts: 'amount must be positive' (P0001) then 'current transaction is aborted' (25P02)
bugs.root_cause: Two test-authoring mistakes: (1) put_action_on_stack's deal_damage_creature builder reads the effect amount from the PAYLOAD, not the card script, so omitting amount raised before the protection check ran. (2) An expected-rejection RPC aborts the whole withRolledBackTx transaction (25P02), so follow-up commands after assert.rejects fail.
bugs.fix: (1) Pass amount in the putOnStack payload. (2) Split the 'legal target still works' assertion into its own test (PT5) so nothing runs after the rejection in PT4.

bugs.error_message: Multiplayer (3-4p Commander): an eliminated player (life 0) was still handed priority and made active player — turn/priority rotations did not skip dead seats.
bugs.root_cause: advance_step and pass_priority rotate by seat_number but never filtered on life_total > 0. In 2p the game ends instantly at one death so it was invisible; in 3-4p the game continues and the dead seat stalled the round / untapped+drew on its turn. maybe_finish_game_session already did last-player-standing correctly.
bugs.fix: Added life_total > 0 filter to the player COUNT (pass-count threshold) in pass_priority and to all four next-seat lookups (next-higher + wrap-to-lowest) across both functions. Reproduced pass_priority from mig 095, advance_step from baseline.

bugs.error_message: DK5 flaky on a fresh DB: imported commander_card_id != cardId('Goblin Raider Test') (two limit-1 lookups picked different duplicate rows).
bugs.root_cause: node --test runs each test file in its own worker process, so ensureTestCards ran concurrently across ~30 workers. The where-not-exists guard races (workers pass the check before any insert commits) -> 11-13 duplicate rows per test-card name. Most tests tolerate dupes (copies are identical), but DK5 compares the importer's limit-1 pick against cardId()'s independent limit-1 pick -> different duplicate rows -> mismatch.
bugs.fix: Serialise seeding with a transaction-scoped advisory lock (pg_advisory_xact_lock) in ensureTestCards: exactly one worker fills the catalog, the rest wait then find rows present and insert nothing. Rebuilt the DB to clear accumulated dupes (now 0).
